title: Implement receiveIf across all client backends
markdown:
## Goal

Implement `receiveIf()` with version tracking across all remaining client backends: PostgresClient, MongoClient, HttpClient, WebSocketClient, IndexedDBClient, and LocalStorageClient. Also update the HTTP server to expose version information and support conditional writes via the existing `/api/v1/receive` endpoint.

## Background

G1-A4-01 added the `receiveIf` method to `NodeProtocolWriteInterface` and implemented it in `MemoryClient`. Now every other client must implement the same interface to maintain protocol conformance.

For database backends (Postgres, Mongo): use database-native atomic compare-and-set. Postgres can use `UPDATE ... WHERE version = $expected RETURNING version`. Mongo can use `findOneAndUpdate` with a version filter.

For transport clients (HTTP, WebSocket): pass `expectedVersion` in the request payload and let the server handle it.

For browser clients (IndexedDB, LocalStorage): implement version tracking in-process, similar to MemoryClient.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `PostgresClient.receiveIf()` uses atomic SQL: `UPDATE ... SET version = version + 1 WHERE version = $expected`
- [ ] `PostgresClient` schema includes a `version INTEGER DEFAULT 1` column (migration added)
- [ ] `PostgresClient.read()` returns `version` in the record
- [ ] `MongoClient.receiveIf()` uses `findOneAndUpdate` with version filter
- [ ] `MongoClient.read()` returns `version` in the record
- [ ] `HttpClient.receiveIf()` sends `expectedVersion` in the POST body to `/api/v1/receive`
- [ ] `WebSocketClient.receiveIf()` includes `expectedVersion` in the WebSocket request payload
- [ ] HTTP server `/api/v1/receive` accepts optional `expectedVersion` and delegates to `client.receiveIf()`
- [ ] HTTP server read endpoint returns `version` in the record response
- [ ] `IndexedDBClient.receiveIf()` implements version tracking in-store
- [ ] `LocalStorageClient.receiveIf()` implements version tracking in-store
- [ ] Existing tests for all clients continue to pass

## Implementation Guide

1. **PostgresClient**: Read `libs/b3nd-client-postgres/mod.ts` and `libs/b3nd-client-postgres/schema.ts`
   - Add `version INTEGER DEFAULT 1` to the schema generation
   - Implement `receiveIf()`: if `expectedVersion` provided, use `UPDATE ... WHERE uri = $1 AND version = $2` then check rowCount
   - Update `receive()` to always increment version
   - Update `read()` to SELECT and return version
2. **MongoClient**: Read `libs/b3nd-client-mongo/mod.ts`
   - Add `version: 1` default to document creation
   - Implement `receiveIf()` with `findOneAndUpdate({ uri, version: expected }, { $inc: { version: 1 } })`
   - Update `read()` to return version
3. **HTTP server**: Read `libs/b3nd-servers/http.ts`
   - In the `/api/v1/receive` handler, extract optional `expectedVersion` from request body
   - If present, call `client.receiveIf()` instead of `client.receive()`
   - In the read handler, include `version` in the JSON response
4. **HttpClient**: Read `libs/b3nd-client-http/mod.ts`
   - Add `receiveIf()` that includes `expectedVersion` in the POST body
5. **WebSocketClient**: Read `libs/b3nd-client-ws/mod.ts`
   - Add `receiveIf()` that includes `expectedVersion` in the payload
6. **IndexedDBClient** and **LocalStorageClient**: follow the MemoryClient pattern (in-process version map)

## Files to Create/Modify

- `libs/b3nd-client-postgres/mod.ts` -- add receiveIf, version column
- `libs/b3nd-client-postgres/schema.ts` -- add version column to schema generation
- `libs/b3nd-client-mongo/mod.ts` -- add receiveIf, version field
- `libs/b3nd-client-http/mod.ts` -- add receiveIf
- `libs/b3nd-client-ws/mod.ts` -- add receiveIf
- `libs/b3nd-client-indexeddb/mod.ts` -- add receiveIf
- `libs/b3nd-client-localstorage/mod.ts` -- add receiveIf
- `libs/b3nd-servers/http.ts` -- accept expectedVersion, return version in reads
- `libs/b3nd-core/types.ts` -- add receiveIf to WebSocketRequest type if needed

## Testing

```bash
# Unit tests (memory, indexeddb, localstorage)
deno test --allow-all --ignore=libs/b3nd-client-postgres,libs/b3nd-client-mongo libs/b3nd-*/

# Integration tests (require running databases)
deno test --allow-all libs/b3nd-client-postgres/
deno test --allow-all libs/b3nd-client-mongo/
```

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: G1-A4-01 (types and MemoryClient implementation)
- **Blocks**: G1-A4-03 (documentation)
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Design `receiveIf(uri, data, { expectedVersion })` â€” conditional writes with optimistic locking
- [ ] Add version tracking to records (or use content hash as version)
- [ ] Update all client implementations to support conditional writes
- [ ] Document concurrency model and recommended patterns
